page: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the server provides 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, backed by 4.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with 75 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes a single complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves a strong single-core CPU performance rating in stress-ng (2929.97), placing it in the top 25%. However, its multi-core CPU performance is weak (5812.19), falling into the bottom 25%, which is typical for shared CPU allocations. Memory bandwidth is generally constrained, with read-only large block speeds at 38.07 GB/sec and write speeds at 34.24 GB/sec. Database and web serving metrics are low, with static web serving throughput at 2.39 GB/sec and database operations scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that require NVMe storage and moderate single-threaded processing but do not demand sustained multi-core performance or high memory density. Given the shared CPU allocation and weak multi-core benchmarks, it is best suited for lightweight applications, basic single-threaded tasks, development environments, or simple utility services rather than high-throughput databases or intensive web serving.
description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While its single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng benchmarks, its shared CPU allocation leads to weak multi-core performance and low database and web serving throughput. This resource profile makes it a cost-efficient option for lightweight single-threaded tasks, development environments, and basic utility services.
